workflows.current_behavior: Telclaude handles private Telegram chat through the relay with Telclaude-owned auth, approvals, memory, provider access, and rollback.
workflows.hermes_target_behavior: Hermes handles the private agent loop behind the Telclaude edge while Telclaude keeps authority, approvals, memory, providers, network containment, and rollback ownership.
workflows.cutover_requirement: All required execution surfaces, network probes, parity fixtures, no-fork proof, and rollback rehearsal must pass from observed evidence before this workflow can flip.

workflows.current_behavior: Telclaude does not yet expose production WhatsApp identity through the Hermes wrapper.
workflows.hermes_target_behavior: A dedicated WhatsApp identity is edge-owned by Telclaude, sanitized into Hermes public or household profiles, and sends outbound replies through Telclaude approval, redaction, budgets, and audit.
workflows.cutover_requirement: WhatsApp ingress, outbound, status, attachment quarantine, identity binding, and direct-Hermes credential denial must pass from edge-mediated shadow evidence.

workflows.current_behavior: Telclaude does not yet expose production email identity through the Hermes wrapper.
workflows.hermes_target_behavior: A dedicated mailbox is edge-owned by Telclaude, sanitized into Hermes public or household profiles, and sends outbound mail through prepared refs, redaction, budgets, and audit.
workflows.cutover_requirement: Email ingress, threading, attachments, outbound send/reply, identity binding, and direct IMAP/SMTP credential denial must pass from edge-mediated shadow evidence.

workflows.current_behavior: AgentMail is not yet wrapped by Telclaude custody for production Hermes use.
workflows.hermes_target_behavior: AgentMail polling or webhook delivery is mediated by Telclaude sidecar or vault custody; Hermes receives sanitized mail events and uses prepared outbound refs only.
workflows.cutover_requirement: AgentMail read/send paths, key custody, allowed-sender policy, thread mapping, and direct key absence in Hermes must pass from edge-mediated shadow evidence.

workflows.current_behavior: Telclaude social identity, queues, and posting approvals remain outside Hermes wrapper cutover evidence.
workflows.hermes_target_behavior: Social timeline, post, reply, and DM events are sanitized into an isolated Hermes public-social profile while credentials, budgets, approvals, and posting audit stay Telclaude-owned.
workflows.cutover_requirement: Timeline ingestion, draft post, approved posting, reply approval, public-memory isolation, and autonomous-budget denial must pass from edge-mediated shadow evidence.

workflows.current_behavior: Family WhatsApp/email assistant flows are not yet represented in Hermes wrapper cutover evidence.
workflows.hermes_target_behavior: Family members use WhatsApp or email through Telclaude edge identity, per-person scopes, consent, household memory, provider release checks, and strong-link provider access.
workflows.cutover_requirement: Contact-only benign tasks, strong-link provider reads, number-only provider denial, private-memory denial, per-recipient reachability, and sensitive-action approval must pass from shadow evidence.

workflows.current_behavior: Bank provider powers remain Telclaude sidecar flows without Hermes wrapper parity evidence.
workflows.hermes_target_behavior: Hermes can use bank reads and prepare actions only through Telclaude-derived MCP/provider tools, sidecar custody, identity scope checks, release policy, and signed write approvals.
workflows.cutover_requirement: Balances, transactions, statements, transfer draft, approved transfer execution, wrong-actor denial, replay denial, and direct provider/vault denial must pass.

workflows.current_behavior: Clalit and health provider powers remain outside Hermes wrapper parity evidence.
workflows.hermes_target_behavior: Hermes can use Clalit/health reads and prepare actions only through Telclaude-derived MCP/provider tools, sidecar custody, identity scope checks, release policy, urgent-language handling, and signed write approvals.
workflows.cutover_requirement: Appointments, lab/referral/medication reads, message drafts, booking prepare/execute, emergency escalation, wrong-actor denial, replay denial, and direct provider/vault denial must pass.

workflows.current_behavior: Government provider powers remain outside Hermes wrapper parity evidence.
workflows.hermes_target_behavior: Hermes can use government status/document reads and prepare submissions only through Telclaude-derived MCP/provider tools, sidecar custody, identity scope checks, release policy, and signed write approvals.
workflows.cutover_requirement: Status lookup, document fetch, form draft, approved submission, wrong-actor denial, replay denial, and direct provider/vault denial must pass.

workflows.current_behavior: Google sidecar powers remain outside Hermes wrapper parity evidence.
workflows.hermes_target_behavior: Hermes can use Gmail, Calendar, Drive, and Contacts reads/prepares only through Telclaude-derived MCP/provider tools, sidecar custody, identity scope checks, release policy, and signed write approvals.
workflows.cutover_requirement: Gmail/Calendar/Drive reads, draft replies/events, approved writes, wrong-actor denial, replay denial, and direct provider/vault denial must pass.

workflows.current_behavior: Telclaude owns cron/background scheduling and approval waits outside Hermes wrapper parity evidence.
workflows.hermes_target_behavior: Long-lived Hermes-backed workflows preserve workflowRunId, initiating actor, authority actor, scope, budget, freshness deadline, side-effect ledger, cancellation, and idempotent resume semantics.
workflows.cutover_requirement: Cron delivery, duplicate suppression, approval-wait resume, provider timeout, cancellation, and rollback-visible run state must pass from observed evidence.

workflows.current_behavior: Browser/computer-use isolation for Hermes wrapper workflows is not yet proven across private, public, household, and provider domains.
workflows.hermes_target_behavior: Browser and computer automation use isolated profiles/displays, no shared cookies, allowed target policies, download/upload quarantine, sidecar custody for sensitive sites, and audited handoffs.
workflows.cutover_requirement: Allowed web research works while direct SMTP/IMAP, WhatsApp bridge, model, vault, provider, metadata, cross-domain, covert egress, and shared-cookie paths fail.
